title: Harden local Restic command surfaces against probe-as-write
goal: Move the active local Restic wrapper scripts under a canonical repository owner and make help, status and unknown-argument behavior fail closed so an inspection command cannot start backup, retention or prune work.

approval.note: Registration only. Runtime replacement or timer changes require a later reviewed authorization.

acceptance.assertion: Active Restic wrapper and unit sources are versioned under one explicit repository owner with install and rollback instructions.
--help and explicit status or plan modes perform no backup, retention, prune, restore or repository mutation.
Unknown arguments fail closed before locks, tunnels, sentinel writes or Restic commands are started.
Tests prove that help and unknown-argument probes cause zero backup-side effects and that normal scheduled execution retains its existing restore gate.
Any later runtime replacement is hash-bound, reversible and separately reviewed before timers or active scripts change.

metadata.incident_summary: A --help probe invoked two scripts that lacked argument parsing, creating encrypted snapshots and running one configured local retention/prune cycle.
